meaning: n.无主物,飘流物,流浪者,流浪儿童,信号旗
examples: The tidings struck a chill to the heart of the poor little waif, and sent a shudder through his frame.
I've been a waif for twenty years!
And his waif Emma looks like she's pregnant, again!
Romain, (right) the kid-waif owners and designers behind the I Love Factory label.
It all depends on how tightly the 1.3-pound waif of a device plans to hold onto its secrets.
